title: Creative Fatigue Half-Life

content.question: How often do you refresh your business's creative? Are you riding dead ads because they were good once?
content.equation: CFH = Time until performance drops to 50% of peak
sop: Log the launch date and peak performance metric (CTR, engagement, or conversion) for your last 10 pieces of creative.
Mark the date each one's performance dropped to half its peak, that's its half-life (see calculation, page 6).
Calculate your average half-life across those 10 pieces.
Build new creative on a cycle shorter than that average half-life, not after performance has already tanked.
Retire or refresh anything currently running past its measured half-life, even if it 'still works.'
content.explainer: Creative has a half-life. Plan the refresh cycle before performance tells you it's time.
questions: How often do you refresh your business's creative? Are you riding dead ads because they were good once?
What's the actual date your best-performing post or ad peaked, and how long ago was that?
If you had to guess your creative's half-life in weeks, would you actually know, or are you flying blind?

definitions.def: Here, the amount of time it takes a piece of content's performance to fall to half of its peak.
Click-Through Rate, the percentage of people who click an ad or link after seeing it.
Industry shorthand for the actual content of an ad, the image, video, or copy itself, as opposed to the targeting or budget behind it.

content.equation_example: Peak performance in Week 1 (100% of peak). Performance drops to 50% of peak by Week 6, so half-life equals 6 weeks. A short half-life (2-3 weeks) means you need a constant creative pipeline. A long half-life (8+ weeks) means you can run winners longer before refreshing.
content.closing_statement: Every ad has an expiration date, even the good ones.

caption:
Principle Nº 92: Creative Fatigue Half-Life

Content performance decays predictably.

Ask yourself: How often do you refresh your business's creative? Are you riding dead ads because they were good once?
